output.summary: Comprehensive audit of ContentWithMedia block against block-type docs and accessibility requirements. Found 6 accessibility gaps (play button missing aria-label, iframes missing title, IconPlay SVG missing aria-hidden, missing headingSize attribute from block-type spec, no prefers-reduced-motion handling, images treated as decorative without configurable alt text). No test page exists; plan defines 14 test scenarios using DemoContainer pattern covering all media types, layouts, dark mode, reversal, video sources, and edge cases.

fileChanges.description: Add accessibility attributes: aria-label on play button, title on iframes, aria-hidden on IconPlay SVG, and alt text support for gallery images
fileChanges.details: 1. Add aria-label='Play video' to the play <button> element (line 195). 2. Add title attribute to YouTube/Vimeo iframes (title='Video player' or similar descriptive text) in both the direct-render iframe (line 231-243) and the JS-injected iframes in ContentWithMedia.js. 3. Ensure the IconPlay SVG is wrapped or output with aria-hidden='true' since the button already has an aria-label. 4. On the single image layout, images are currently rendered via wp_get_attachment_image which uses the media library alt text by default - verify this is populated or provide a fallback.

fileChanges.description: Add accessibility attributes matching PHP changes: aria-label on play button, aria-hidden on IconPlay
fileChanges.details: 1. Add aria-label={__('Play video', 'takt')} to the play <button> element (around line 531). 2. Add aria-hidden='true' to the <IconPlay /> component usage (line 553). 3. Add title attribute to iframe elements injected in the playVideo function for YouTube/Vimeo embeds.

fileChanges.description: Add title attribute to dynamically injected YouTube/Vimeo iframes
fileChanges.details: Both the YouTube and Vimeo iframe strings generated in the click handler (lines 30-33) need a title attribute: title='Video player'. This is required for WCAG 2.1 SC 4.1.2 (iframes must have accessible names).

fileChanges.description: Add missing headingSize attribute per block-type documentation requirements
fileChanges.details: The block-type doc specifies a headingSize attribute ('small' -> H3, 'regular' -> H2) but it is not currently in block.json. Add: "headingSize": { "type": "string", "default": "regular", "enum": ["small", "regular"] }. The TSX and PHP already hardcode headingSize=2; this change makes it configurable per the block-type spec.

attributeChanges.name: headingSize
attributeChanges.description: Heading level: 'small' renders H3, 'regular' renders H2. Per block-type documentation section 5 (Heading Size Mapping).

designDecisions.question: Should the headingSize attribute be added as specified in the block-type doc, or is the hardcoded H2 intentional?
designDecisions.recommendation: Add headingSize attribute. The block-type doc explicitly defines this mapping (small=H3, regular=H2) and existing code already passes headingSize to ThemeHeading. Making it configurable follows the documented pattern and allows editors to maintain proper heading hierarchy.

designDecisions.question: Gallery images have no user-configurable alt text - they use whatever is set in the WordPress media library. Is this sufficient?
designDecisions.recommendation: This is acceptable because WordPress's wp_get_attachment_image() uses the alt text from the media library entry. No block-level alt attribute is needed as long as editors set alt text in the media library. Document this expectation in the test plan.

accessibilityGaps.requirement: Play button must have an accessible name (aria-label) - buttons with only icon content need aria-label per accessibility.md section 3
accessibilityGaps.fix: Add aria-label='Play video' to the <button> element in both PHP and TSX. The button currently contains only an image and an icon with no text.

accessibilityGaps.requirement: IconPlay SVG must have aria-hidden='true' per accessibility.md section 3 (SVG Icons)
accessibilityGaps.fix: Add aria-hidden='true' to the <IconPlay /> component in TSX, and add aria-hidden='true' to the SVG output in PHP (theme_block_asset output). The icon is decorative since the button will have an aria-label.

accessibilityGaps.requirement: Iframes must have a title attribute for accessible name (WCAG 2.1 SC 4.1.2)
accessibilityGaps.fix: Add title='YouTube video player' or title='Vimeo video player' to all <iframe> elements - in PHP static rendering, in TSX playVideo() function, and in ContentWithMedia.js frontend click handler.

accessibilityGaps.requirement: Heading hierarchy must be maintained - headingSize attribute allows appropriate level per context (block-type doc section 5)
accessibilityGaps.fix: Add headingSize attribute to block.json with enum ['small', 'regular']. Update TSX to pass headingSize={attributes.headingSize === 'small' ? 3 : 2} to ThemeHeading. Update PHP similarly. Currently hardcoded to H2.

accessibilityGaps.requirement: Respect prefers-reduced-motion for video playback transitions (accessibility.md section 5)
accessibilityGaps.fix: The play button uses group-hover transitions (transition-colors on the icon span in the video-playback pattern). While the current implementation does not have heavy animations, verify that the CSS transitions respect prefers-reduced-motion. The theme may already handle this globally, but it should be verified during QA.

accessibilityGaps.requirement: Images should have appropriate alt text or be marked decorative (accessibility.md section 3, block-type doc section 6)
accessibilityGaps.fix: Gallery images rendered via wp_get_attachment_image use the media library alt text. If no alt text is set in the media library, images will have empty alt. This is acceptable for decorative images but problematic for informative ones. Document that editors should set alt text in the media library. No code change required - this is an editorial guidance issue.

testPageScenarios.title: Full Content - Gallery Layout (Default)
testPageScenarios.description: All fields populated: eyebrow, heading, description, 2 buttons, 3 gallery images. Default layout (media left, content right). Tests complete rendering with all optional fields.

interactiveTests.name: Video play button triggers video playback
interactiveTests.expect: Play button hides, video begins playing or iframe is injected into embed-video-container

implementationGaps.description: Play button has no aria-label. The button contains only an image (poster) and an SVG icon with no text content.
implementationGaps.fix: Add aria-label='Play video' to the button in both TSX and PHP.

implementationGaps.description: IconPlay SVG lacks aria-hidden='true'. Without it, screen readers may announce the SVG content.
implementationGaps.fix: Add aria-hidden='true' to IconPlay SVG element or component.

implementationGaps.description: YouTube/Vimeo iframes have no title attribute. Iframes require an accessible name per WCAG 2.1 SC 4.1.2.
implementationGaps.fix: Add title='Video player' to iframes in PHP template, TSX playVideo(), and ContentWithMedia.js.

implementationGaps.description: headingSize attribute specified in block-type doc is not implemented. Block always renders H2 regardless of context.
implementationGaps.fix: Add headingSize attribute to block.json, update TSX and PHP to map 'small' -> 3, 'regular' -> 2 for ThemeHeading.

implementationGaps.description: Single image layout in PHP always uses rounded-3xl even in dark mode, while gallery layout switches to rounded-xl in dark mode. TSX has the same behavior. This may be intentional but is inconsistent.
implementationGaps.fix: Verify with design whether single image should also use rounded-xl in dark mode. If so, update both TSX and PHP.

implementationGaps.description: prefers-reduced-motion: The block uses transition-colors on play button hover state. The theme may handle this globally, but should be verified.
implementationGaps.fix: Verify global prefers-reduced-motion handling during QA. No code change expected.
